sources.title: WADA Prohibited List - Thymosin beta-4 and derivatives e.g. TB-500
Progress on the Function and Application of Thymosin β4

claims.statement: WADA lists thymosin-β4 and its derivatives, including TB-500, among prohibited peptide hormones/growth factors-related substances in sport.

context.population: competitive athletes subject to anti-doping rules

extractedEvidence.quote: Thymosin-β4 and its derivatives e.g. TB-500
extractedEvidence.pageOrSection: Peptide hormones, growth factors, related substances, and mimetics

reviewFlags: sports-regulatory claim; jurisdiction/league-specific guidance should be reviewed

claims.statement: Much published biomedical literature concerns thymosin beta-4 or formulations studied in specific clinical contexts; BioStack should not assume vendor TB-500 products are equivalent to the parent protein or studied formulations.

context.population: users extrapolating thymosin beta-4 research to TB-500 products

extractedEvidence.pageOrSection: Review of thymosin beta-4 functions and applications

reviewFlags: do not generalize parent-protein literature to gray-market TB-500

claims.statement: Online recovery and injury-repair claims for TB-500 are often broader than the available human evidence for specific products; BioStack should treat this as an evidence gap unless claim-specific human evidence or a dedicated misinformation-monitoring source is added.
reviewFlags: do not publish as proven human benefit

reviewReasons: Pilot misinformation-heavy evidence packet requires human review before publication.
